task_completion.description: The docker-compose.yml file is refactored to use environment variables with default values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing `docker-compose.yml` containing `${VARIABLE:-default}` syntax for the 5 specified fields. The defaults must be: 14 (Postgres image tag), dev_password (Postgres password), 18-slim (Node.js image tag), 3000 (Host port), and development (App Mode).

task_completion.description: The .env.prod file is created with the specified production values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of `.env.prod` assigning the production values (15, secure_prod_pass_2024, 20-slim, 80, production) to the corresponding variables used in docker-compose.yml.

task_completion.description: The Docker stack is deployed using the .env.prod file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of a docker compose command that explicitly uses the .env.prod file (e.g., `docker compose --env-file .env.prod up -d`).

task_completion.description: The containers are running with the production settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing `docker ps` or similar command confirming the containers are running with the production settings (e.g., host port 80 mapped to 3000, postgres:15 image), or a browser showing the app successfully loaded on localhost:80.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the docker-compose.yml and created .env.prod in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The files are saved in the text editor or verified via terminal commands like `cat` or `ls` in the `~/projects/inventory-system` directory.

integrity.description: The docker-compose.yml file does not have the production values hardcoded directly into it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The `docker-compose.yml` file must use the `${VAR:-default}` syntax with the development defaults, not the production values.

integrity.description: The agent actually deployed the stack using Docker Compose.
integrity.visual_evidence: Genuine terminal execution and output of Docker Compose commands, rather than just echoing text or running standalone docker containers.
